response.justification: Bogdan's career is built almost entirely on web and TypeScript/Node.js development with no meaningful C++ depth — his only C/C++ exposure is a six-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, and C++ does not even appear in his listed skills. The role's core requirements (C++ with STL, multi-threading, industrial networking, IEC 62443, MQTT, and industrial protocols such as OPC UA or Modbus) are unmet. While he demonstrates solid architectural thinking and event-driven design experience, these are expressed in languages and domains (web, DLang, TypeScript) entirely disconnected from industrial automation and on-premises embedded platform work.
strengths: Demonstrated event-driven and distributed-systems architecture experience at scale (Sociomantic, GISCollective)
Active mentoring and knowledge-sharing record through workshops, code katas, and conference talks
Exposure to low-latency, high-throughput backend systems using async I/O and caching strategies
concerns: C++ is essentially absent from his skill set — no listing, and only ~6 months of exposure more than ten years ago
Zero industrial automation, IoT, or IEC 62443 / SDL security standards experience
Entire career trajectory is web/cloud/TypeScript, making a pivot to industrial on-premises C++ platform work highly implausible
